documentation:
# Murfey

A transporter for data from Diamond eBIC microscope and detector machines onto the Diamond network.

## Who is Murfey?

Murfey, the package, is named after [Eliza Murfey, the inventor](https://nationalrrmuseum.org/blog/mother-of-invention-women-railroad-innovators/):

> Focusing on the mechanics of the railroad, inventor Eliza Murfey created devices designed
> to improve the bearings on rail wheels. With sixteen patents for her lubrication system,
> it was Murfey who designed the packings that would lubricate the axles with oil, aiding
> in the reduction of derailments caused by seized axles and bearings.

## How do I set up a development environment?

We suggest you start with your favourite virtual environment (mamba/conda/python virtualenv/...),
then install using the following command.

```text
$ git clone git@github.com:DiamondLightSource/python-murfey.git
$ cd python-murfey
$ pip install -e .[client,server,developer]
```

The packages included under the `[developer]` installation key contain some helpful tools to aid you with developing Murfey further:

- `bump-my-version` - Simplifies version control.
- `ipykernel` - Enables interactive code development via Jupyter Notebooks.
- `pre-commit` - Allows for the installation and running of hooks to help with linting, formatting, and type checking your code.
- `pytest` - Used in conjunction with test functions to evaluate the reliability of your code.

Instructions for setting up the database for Murfey to register files to can be found [here](src/murfey/server/README.md).

Finally, you may want to set up an ISPyB mock database server and a Zocalo
development environment. The instructions for this are out of scope here.

You can then start the Murfey server with

```text
$ murfey.server
```

and connect the client with

```text
$ murfey --server http://127.0.0.1:8000
```

You can also install a client on a remote machine. This machine only needs to have
a minimum Python installation and curl. Open the murfey server website in a browser
on the client, and navigate to the bootstrap page. Then copy the displayed commands
into a command line terminal.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.clem.validate_and_sanitise
documentation:
Performs validation and sanitisation on the incoming file paths, ensuring that
no forbidden characters are present and that the the path points only to allowed
sections of the file server.

Returns the file path as a sanitised string that can be converted into a Path
object again.

NOTE: Due to the instrument name query, 'db' now needs to be passed as an
explicit variable to this function from within a FastAPI endpoint, as using the
instance that was imported directly won't load it in the correct state.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.clem.get_db_entry
documentation:
Searches the CLEM workflow-related tables in the Murfey database for an entry that
matches the file path or series name within a given session. Returns the entry if
a match is found, otherwise register it as a new entry in the database.

variables.name: murfey.server.api.clem.valid_file_types
documentation: HELPER FUNCTIONS

modules.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap
documentation:
API endpoints related to installing Murfey on a client.

Client machines may not have a direct internet connection, so Murfey allows
passing through download requests for Cygwin and MSYS2 to their respective
websites, and requests for Python packages to PyPI using the PEP 503 simple
API.

A static HTML page gives instructions on how to install on a network-isolated
system that has Python already installed. A previously set up system does not
need to have pip installed in order to bootstrap Murfey. Python and rsync are
required.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_bootstrap_instructions
documentation:
Return a website containing instructions for installing the Murfey client on a
machine with no internet access.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_pip_wheel
documentation:
Return a static version of pip. This does not need to be the newest or best,
but has to be compatible with all supported Python versions.
This is only used during bootstrapping by the client to identify and then
download the actually newest appropriate version of pip.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_murfey_wheel
documentation:
Return a wheel file containing the latest release version of Murfey. We should
not have to worry about the exact Python compatibility here, as long as
murfey.bootstrap is compatible with all relevant versions of Python.
This also ignores yanked releases, which again should be fine.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_cygwin_setup
documentation:
Obtain and pass through a Cygwin installer from an official source.
This is used during client bootstrapping and can download and install the
Cygwin distribution that then remains on the client machines.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.find_cygwin_mirror
documentation:
Find an appropriate Cygwin mirror to pass download requests through to.
We don't expect these to change often, so we only do this once during the
lifetime of the server.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.parse_cygwin_request
documentation: Forward a Cygwin setup request to an official mirror.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_msys2_setup
documentation:
Obtain and pass through an MSYS2 installer from an official source.
This is used during client bootstrapping, and can download and install the
MSYS2 distribution that then remains on the client machines.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_msys2_main_index
documentation:
Returns a simple index displaying valid MSYS2 systems and the latest setup file
from the main MSYS2 repository.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_msys2_environment_index
documentation:
Returns a list of all MSYS2 environments for a given system from the main MSYS2
repository.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_msys2_package_index
documentation:
Obtain a list of all available MSYS2 packages for a given environment from the main
MSYS2 repo.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_msys2_package_file
documentation: Obtain and pass through a specific download for an MSYS2 package.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_number_of_github_pages
documentation:
Parses the main GitHub releases page to find the number of pages present in the
repository.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_windows_terminal_releases
documentation: Returns a list of stable Windows Terminal releases from the GitHub repository.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_windows_terminal_version_assets
documentation: Returns a list of packages for the selected version of Windows Terminal.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_windows_terminal_package_file
documentation: Returns a package from the GitHub repository.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap._get_full_pypi_path_response
documentation:
Validates the package name, sanitises it if valid, and attempts to return a HTTP
response from PyPI.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_pypi_index
documentation: Obtain list of all PyPI packages via the simple API (PEP 503).

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_pypi_package_downloads_list
documentation:
Obtain list of all package downloads from PyPI via the simple API (PEP 503), and
rewrite all download URLs to point to this server, under the current directory.

functions.name: murfey.server.api.bootstrap.get_pypi_file
documentation: Obtain and pass through a specific download for a PyPI package.

A method to stop the server. This should be removed before Murfey is
deployed in production. To remove it we need to figure out how to control
to process (eg. systemd) and who to run it as.

modules.name: murfey.client.contexts.clem
documentation:
Provides instructions to the server side on how different file types associated with
the CLEM workflow should be processed.

functions.name: murfey.client.contexts.clem._file_transferred_to
documentation: Returns the Path of the transferred file on the DLS file system.

functions.name: murfey.client.contexts.clem._get_source
documentation: Returns the Path of the file on the client PC.

functions.name: murfey.client.contexts.clem._get_image_elements
documentation:
Searches the XML metadata recursively to find the nodes tagged as "Element" that
have image-related tags. Some LIF datasets have layers of nested elements, so a
recursive approach is needed to avoid certain datasets breaking it.

methods.name: murfey.client.contexts.clem.CLEMContext.register_lif_file
documentation:
Constructs the URL and dictionary to be posted to the server, which will then
register the LIF file in the database correctly as part of the CLEM workflow.

methods.name: murfey.client.contexts.clem.CLEMContext.process_lif_file
documentation:
Constructs the URL and dictionary to be posted to the server, which will then
trigger the preprocessing of the LIF file.

methods.name: murfey.client.contexts.clem.CLEMContext.register_tiff_file
documentation:
Constructs the URL and dictionary to be posted to the server, which will then
register the TIFF file in the database correctly as part of the CLEM workflow.

methods.name: murfey.client.contexts.clem.CLEMContext.process_tiff_series
documentation:
Constructs the URL and dictionary to be posted to the server, which will then
trigger the preprocessing of this instance of a TIFF series.

modules.name: murfey.client.rsync
documentation:
rsync is a Linux process that does fast, versatile remote (and local) file copying.
This module allows Murfey to use rsync to facilitate its file transfers, and to
parse the logs outputted by rsync in order confirm that the file has been correctly
transferred.

classes.name: murfey.client.rsync.TransferResult
documentation: $ rsync -riiv . --times --outbuf=line wra62962@ws133:/dls/tmp/wra62962/junk --dry-run
sending incremental file list
.f          README.md
<f.st...... large
.f          tests/util/__pycache__/test_state.cpython-39-pytest-6.2.5.pyc
<f+++++++++ tests/server/test_main.py
sent 3,785 bytes  received 355 bytes  2,760.00 bytes/sec
total size is 314,923,092  speedup is 76,068.38 (DRY RUN)

methods.name: murfey.client.rsync.RSyncer._transfer
documentation:
Transfer files via an rsync sub-process, and parses the rsync stdout to verify
the success of the transfer.

functions.name: murfey.client.update.check
documentation:
Verify that the current client version can run against the selected server.
If the version number is outside the allowed range then this can trigger
an update on the client, and in that case will terminate the process.

functions.name: murfey.client.update.install_murfey
documentation:
Install a specific version of the Murfey client.
Return 'true' on success and 'false' on error.

modules.name: murfey.client.watchdir
documentation:
Watches the specified directory, crawling through it recursively to identify potential
files for transfer, and notifies listening processes to perform the transfer on files
that are ready.

methods.name: murfey.client.watchdir.DirWatcher.scan
documentation:
Scans the specified directory and its subdirectories recursively for files, and
compiles of a list of files to send for transfer.

methods.name: murfey.client.watchdir.DirWatcher._notify_for_transfer
documentation:
Perform a Boolean check to see if a file is ready to be transferred, and
removes it from the file candidates list.

methods.name: murfey.client.watchdir.DirWatcher._scan_directory
documentation:
Explores the specified directory and its subdirectories recursively to identify
files for potential transfer, returning them as dictionary entries.

functions.name: murfey.client._enable_webbrowser_in_cygwin
documentation: Helper function to make webbrowser.open() work in CygWin

modules.name: murfey.client.tui.progress
documentation:
A progressbar widget that uses block characters rather than a thin
horizontal line. This makes it more visible on Windows.

methods.name: murfey.client.tui.progress.BlockProgressBar._get_pulse_segments
documentation:
Get a list of segments to render a pulse animation.

Returns:
    List[Segment]: A list of segments, one segment per character.

methods.name: murfey.client.tui.progress.BlockBarColumn.render
documentation: Gets a progress bar widget for a task.

methods.name: murfey.client.customlogging.CustomHandler.__init__
documentation: Set up a handler instance, record the callback function.

modules.name: murfey.client.analyser
documentation:
Contains functions for analysing the various types of files hauled by Murfey, and
assigning to them the correct contexts (CLEM, SPA, tomography, etc.) for processing
on the server side.

Individual contexts can be found in murfey.client.contexts.

methods.name: murfey.client.analyser.Analyser._find_extension
documentation: Identifies the file extension and stores that information in the class.

methods.name: murfey.client.analyser.Analyser._find_context
documentation:
Using various conditionals, identifies what workflow the file is part of, and
assigns the correct context class to that batch of rsync files for subsequent
stages of processing. Actions to take for individual files will be determined
in the Context classes themselves.

methods.name: murfey.client.tui.app.MurfeyTUI._increment_transferred_files_prometheus
documentation: Prometheus can handle higher traffic so update for every transferred file rather
than batching as we do for the Murfey database updates in _increment_transferred_files

functions.name: murfey.cli.dlq_resubmit.handle_failed_posts
documentation: Deal with any messages that have been sent as failed client posts

functions.name: murfey.cli.dlq_resubmit.handle_dlq_messages
documentation: Reinjected to the queue

functions.name: murfey.cli.dlq_resubmit.run
documentation:
Method of checking and purging murfey queues on rabbitmq
Two types of messages are possible:
- failed client posts which need reposting to the murfey server API
- feedback messages that can be sent back to rabbitmq

classes.name: murfey.util.state.State
documentation:
A helper class to coordinate shared state across server instances.
This is a Mapping with added (synchronous) set and delete functionality,
as well as asynchronous .update/.delete calls. It implements the Observer
pattern notifying synchronous and asynchronous callback functions.

methods.name: murfey.util.rsync.RsyncPipe._run_rsync
documentation:
Run rsync -v on a list of files using procrunner.

```
param root

root path of files for transferring; structure below the root is preserved

type root

pathlib.Path object

param files

List of files to be transferred

type files

list of strigs or pathlib.Path objects

param destination

Directory that files are to be copied to.

type destination

string or pathlib.Path object

param retry

If True put failed files back into the queue to be consumed

type retry

bool
```

methods.name: murfey.util.rsync.RsyncPipe._parse_rsync_stdout
documentation:
Parse rsync stdout to collect information such as the paths of transferred
files and the amount of data transferred.

```
param stdout

stdout of rsync process

type stdout

bytes
```

methods.name: murfey.util.rsync.RsyncPipe._parse_rsync_stderr
documentation:
Parse rsync stderr to collect information on any files that failed to transfer.

```
param stderr

stderr of rsync process

type stderr

bytes
```

functions.name: murfey.util.posix_path
documentation:
Converts a Windows-style path into a Posix one. Used primarily when running
subproceses in bash terminals on Windows devices, which can only accept
Posix paths.

Returns it as a string because this path won't be recognised as an existing
path when converted to a Path object.

classes.name: murfey.util.Observer

methods.name: murfey.util.Observer.__init__
documentation: The class here should be derived from typing.Generic[P]
with P = ParamSpec("P"), and the notify/anotify functions should use
*args: P.args, **kwargs: P.kwargs.
However, ParamSpec is Python 3.10+ (PEP 612), so we can't use that yet.

A helper class implementing the observer pattern supporting both
synchronous and asynchronous notification calls and both synchronous and
asynchronous callback functions.

of the sessions that Murfey is overseeing, along with the relationships between them.

modules.name: murfey.workflows.clem.register_preprocessing_results
documentation:
Functions to process the requests received by Murfey related to the CLEM workflow.

The CLEM-related file registration API endpoints can eventually be moved here, since
the file registration processes all take place on the server side only.

functions.name: murfey.workflows.clem.register_preprocessing_results._validate_and_sanitise
documentation:
Performs validation and sanitisation on the incoming file paths, ensuring that
no forbidden characters are present and that the the path points only to allowed
sections of the file server.

Returns the file path as a sanitised string that can be converted into a Path
object again.

NOTE: Due to the instrument name query, 'db' now needs to be passed as an
explicit variable to this function from within a FastAPI endpoint, as using the
instance that was imported directly won't load it in the correct state.

functions.name: murfey.workflows.clem.register_preprocessing_results.get_db_entry
documentation:
Searches the CLEM workflow-related tables in the Murfey database for an entry that
matches the file path or series name within a given session. Returns the entry if
a match is found, otherwise register it as a new entry in the database.

functions.name: murfey.workflows.clem.register_preprocessing_results.register_lif_preprocessing_result
documentation:
session_id (recipe)
register (wrapper)
result (wrapper)
    key1
    key2
    ...

modules.name: util.test_lif
documentation: Contains unit tests for lif.py.
